title: Queue Wait Time Spike — Auto Loans Up 340% in 2 Hours
description: Average wait time jumped 340% in the auto loans queue over the last 2 hours. 127 members currently waiting. Correlates with a 2.9% rate promotion email sent this morning via Marketing Cloud to 45,000 members at 7:15 AM — not flagged to Contact Center ops for staffing preparation.

confidence.rationale: Marketing Cloud send-time and Dynamics 365 queue spike align within 8 minutes — high causal confidence.

title: Agent Attrition Cluster — Friday Evening Shift at Risk
description: 3 agents submitted PTO for Friday evening shift. Historical data shows this shift already runs at 85% force-to-load. Predicted service level drop to 62% without intervention. This shift handles 22% of weekly mortgage servicing volume due to West Coast members calling after work.

confidence.rationale: Force-to-load and PTO counts are direct telemetry; 62% projection has ±4 pp variance against last 9 Friday-evening events.

title: Sentiment Decline — Mortgage Servicing Up 28% Negative This Week
description: Negative sentiment in mortgage servicing calls increased 28% this week. Dynamics 365 sentiment analysis flags 'payment portal' and 'escrow' as top negative keywords. Cross-referencing with IT incident log shows a portal outage 48 hours ago — the root cause Dynamics 365 alone cannot surface.

confidence.rationale: Sentiment timeline aligns with IT incident timeline within 14 minutes; keyword detection precision 0.91 on this volume.
